task.name: Backend task queue: reconcile, list, consume
task.objective: Make the backend task queue truthful and actionable: (1) RECONCILE — settle every locally-consumed task whose goal finished (goal done → task resolved, goal failed → task failed) via task-update-status; (2) LIST — render all unresolved tasks; (3) CONSUME (opt-in) — claim tasks and convert them into taskvisor goals, recording the task↔goal mapping so a later run can reconcile them. The lifecycle contract: task-claim is ALWAYS called before any work is taken, and task-update-status ALWAYS marks the outcome.

glossary.term: "list" (default) or "consume" — set by a `consume` token in $ARGUMENTS.
Consume count: integer following the `consume` token. Default 1, hard cap 10. A full-scope phase chain counts as ONE consume unit (the cap never truncates a chain mid-way).

Optional `category=<c>` and `severity=<s>` tokens in $ARGUMENTS. Validate against the closed sets (category: plan, supervisor, validator, execute, general; severity: critical, warning, info) and reject unknown values naming the allowed ones.
.tmux-cli/task-goals.yaml — the local task↔goal ledger: `mappings: [{task_id, goal_id, title, claimed_at}]`. Created on first consume; absence means nothing to reconcile.
.tmux-cli/goals.yaml — read-only here (atomic-rename writes make unlocked reads safe). NEVER written by this command.

lane-gate.name: solo-lane
lane-gate.title: Solo-lane gate (LANE GATE G1–G5)
lane-gate.decision-rule: ALL of G1–G4 must hold AND the demotion veto must not fire → lane=solo; any miss or a fired veto → full. Evaluated UPSTREAM only; executing agents never self-declare.
lane-gate.criterion: Exactly one unit of work (single backend task at consume; exactly 1 pending task at plan step 3d).
Every green-state check is a deterministic bare command (grep/test/build) — verification needs no semantic judgment.
Localized edit: no new files, no new public API/schema; expected change touches <=2 existing files (estimated from proposed_fix / spec).
Not critical: task severity in {info, warning} / priority below critical tier.
Runtime demotion (statemachine.go — informational here, enforced in Go): solo honored only on validation cycle 1 — any validation failure, stuck recovery, or retry permanently demotes to full.
lane-gate.veto: A solo verdict additionally requires goal.md `## Lane` != full (and the goal's goals.yaml entry lane != full when readable). Either surface reading `full` is a prior-G5-demotion record: the veto fires and the verdict is full regardless of G1–G4 — a demoted goal can never re-enter solo. Absent/unreadable Lane data does NOT fire the veto (it blocks recorded demotions; it is not an affirmation — the fails-closed rule below applies to criteria, not to V).
lane-gate.evaluation-sources: Consume step 3.2 → severity / proposed_fix / the derived validate commands. Plan step 3d → G1 by branch condition; G2 from goal.md `## Validation Rules` + `## Investigation Config` (every investigator must be command-type/pure-command — semantic types like code-review miss G2); G3 from module scope in the research context; G4 from the goal's goals.yaml entry priority < 10 (read-only; atomic-rename writes make unlocked reads safe), falling back to goal.md `## Lane` == solo (consume already passed G4); neither readable → G4 fails closed → full. Veto V → goal.md `## Lane` + the goal's goals.yaml entry lane (read-only; atomic-rename writes make unlocked reads safe). Missing data → criterion fails closed. The staleness gate (consume substep 3.2) runs BEFORE G1–G4 — a stale task is failed and dropped before any lane evaluation.
lane-gate.note: Creation-seam enforcement is enum-only plus two named machine cross-checks in goal-create (internal/mcp tools_taskvisor.go GoalCreate): REJECT lane=solo with an empty validate list (G2 proxy) and WARN-LOG a lane=solo scope spanning >1 top-level directory (G3 proxy). Everything else in G1–G4 remains operator-evaluated convention at the two entry points.

investigate.xml step 2f's inline fast-path is lane-INDEPENDENT by design (B9b): ANY all-pure-command RERUN set inlines in-window, full lane included (goal-061 post-mortem, internal/taskvisor/inline.go). inv_spawned=0 on a full-lane goal whose rerun investigators are all pure-command is EXPECTED behavior, not a lane leak.

llm.mandate: NEVER take work without claiming first — task-claim precedes goal-create, no exceptions
NEVER write GOALS_FILE — goals are created ONLY via the goal-create MCP tool; the only file this command writes is MAPPING_FILE
NEVER mark a task resolved unless its mapped goal Status is "done" in GOALS_FILE — resolution is earned by the validated goal, not assumed
NEVER retry-loop a failed status update — 403/404/422 means another actor advanced the task; drop the mapping with a warning and move on
Consume is opt-in and capped at 10 per invocation — never drain the queue

Consume is FULLY AUTONOMOUS — NEVER prompt or wait for user input (no AskUserQuestion, no blocking prompt) anywhere in the consume + auto-start flow; every claimed task reaches a terminal/mapped state via the deterministic rules here (consume is fully autonomous, never prompt or wait)

step.title: Parse arguments and preflight
step.action: Tokenize $ARGUMENTS; extract MODE, N, FILTERS per the glossary. Unknown tokens → print usage and STOP.
Read .tmux-cli/setting.yaml and resolve API enablement (flat `apiEnabled` wins over nested `api.enabled`).

check.action: Print: "Task API is disabled — set api.enabled: true in .tmux-cli/setting.yaml and retry."

step.title: Reconcile (always runs, before listing)
step.note: FALLBACK actor: the taskvisor daemon now PUSH-resolves a mapped backend task at the moment its goal reaches a durable terminal state (goal→done ⇒ resolved, goal→failed / blocked_by=convergence-circuit-breaker ⇒ failed) and atomically drops the entry from MAPPING_FILE. Reconcile here is the FALLBACK that settles everything the push did not — offline/API-down runs, daemon crashes, and cascade-failed downstream goals — so it must keep reading the same `{task_id, goal_id, title, claimed_at}` ledger shape the daemon writes. Both actors are idempotent: a push that already resolved a task simply leaves no mapping for reconcile to see.
step.action: Read MAPPING_FILE. Absent or empty → skip to step 2.
For each mapping entry, find its goal_id in GOALS_FILE and decide:

branch.action: Call task-update-status with id=task_id, status=resolved, resolution={goal_id, finished_at from the goal, summary: "goal validated and completed"}.
On success: remove the mapping entry; record "task {task_id} → resolved (goal {goal_id} done)".

branch.condition: goal Status == failed, OR Status == blocked with blocked_by == convergence-circuit-breaker
branch.action: Call task-update-status with id=task_id, status=failed, resolution={goal_id, reason: the goal's failed_by/blocked_by value}.
On success: remove the mapping entry; record "task {task_id} → failed (goal {goal_id} {reason})".
Keep the mapping; record as in-flight for the step-2 table.
Remove the mapping and print: "WARNING: goal {goal_id} no longer exists — task {task_id} left in its current backend state; handle manually via task-update-status."

check.action: Another actor advanced the task. Remove the mapping, print a one-line warning with the error, and continue with the next entry — never retry.

step.action: Rewrite MAPPING_FILE with the surviving entries (write the full file once, after the loop).
Print a reconcile summary line: "Reconciled: {r} resolved, {f} failed, {k} in-flight, {d} dropped." (omit when all four are 0).

step.title: List unresolved tasks
step.action: The backend filters by a single status per call. Compose "unresolved" from three task-list MCP calls: status=new, status=claimed, status=in_progress — each with FILTERS applied and limit=50.
Merge the pages, de-duplicate by id, and sort: severity (critical > warning > info), then created_at ascending (oldest first).
Use description_preview only — never call task-get for rows the user did not ask about.
Print totals per status; when any call reports total > limit, print: "more available — narrow with category=/severity= filters".

check.condition: all three calls return zero tasks
check.action: Print: "No unresolved tasks." Then STOP (consume mode has nothing to claim either).

step.title: Consume — claim and convert to goals (MODE == consume only)
step.action: Repeat up to N times (stop early when nothing claimable). ONE iteration = one claimed PRIMARY task PLUS its full materialized full-scope chain (substep 3.2's FULL-SCOPE DEFAULT + chain-commit) — the chain counts as a SINGLE consume unit and is never truncated mid-way by the cap:

substep.title: Drain deferred tasks — re-check readiness BEFORE the repeat loop
substep.note: Runs ONCE before the repeat loop. The deferred ledger .tmux-cli/deferred-tasks.yaml (`deferred: [{task_id, depends_on, deferred_at}]`) holds tasks this machine claimed on an earlier run but could not consume because a prerequisite was unresolved. Absent ledger → skip this substep entirely. This substep is what makes a deferred not-ready task "consumed on a later run", and is the un-stranding mechanism for a claimed-but-not-ready task: there is no unclaim primitive, so the entry is reprocessed by id, never re-claimed.
substep.action: For each ledger entry, call task-get(task_id) and re-check readiness: prefer the `ready` field; else resolve from `depends_on` (every prerequisite must be resolved/terminal). Now-ready → run it through substep 3.2 DIRECTLY (it is ALREADY claimed by this machine — do NOT re-claim) and remove its entry from the ledger. Still-not-ready → leave the entry queued for a future drain. task-get 404 (task vanished) → drop the entry with a warning. Draining is deterministic (ledger read + per-entry decision + ledger rewrite); it NEVER prompts or waits for a human.

substep.title: Claim BEFORE any work
substep.note: task-claim runs a zombie-reconcile preflight FIRST (before the task-225 git-freshness gate and before claiming): any goal stuck `running` whose worker tmux session died while the daemon stayed alive is auto re-pended (or failed if its retry budget is spent) so it stops head-of-line-blocking the queue — startup crash-recovery never re-fires for a live daemon whose session died, so the claim path self-heals it. The preflight is best-effort and reuses the exact crash-recovery liveness/cleanup logic; it never blocks a claim, never increments retries, and runs before git-freshness so local self-healing happens even on a diverged checkout (reconcile is neither a claim nor a dispatch, so "nothing claimed on refusal" still holds).

check.action: Print: "Nothing claimable (filters: {FILTERS})." and exit the consume loop.

substep.action: Use the returned task object verbatim — id, title, description, proposed_fix, expected_green_state, severity.

readiness-gate.title: READINESS GATE — defer a not-ready claimed task; NEVER convert it into a running goal
readiness-gate.action: Re-check the claimed task's readiness before any conversion: prefer the `ready` field on the claimed task; if absent, call task-get(id) and resolve readiness from its `depends_on` (every prerequisite must be resolved/terminal). If NOT ready (`ready==false`, or any unresolved prerequisite), DEFER — never goal-create, never fail/drop: append {task_id, depends_on, deferred_at} to .tmux-cli/deferred-tasks.yaml, log `deferred: task {id} not-ready — prerequisite(s) {ids} unresolved; consumed on a later run`, and `continue` WITHOUT counting against N. The task stays claimed (no unclaim primitive — same convention as the goal-create-failure path); substep 3.0's drain revisits it, so it is never stranded claimed-but-unconsumed. A not-ready task is NEVER converted into a running goal — readiness gates conversion, and an unmet/unresolved prerequisite means defer, not run.

substep.title: Convert to a goal via goal-create

params.param: expected_green_state split into individually checkable items, plus: "Backend task {id} is satisfied: {expected_green_state first line}".

Concrete bare commands (no shell wrappers, no && chains), derived in priority order: (1) backticked/recognizable commands inside expected_green_state, (2) commands inside proposed_fix, (3) a minimal deterministic check the green state implies (a test run or a grep against the named artifact), (4) BUILD+TEST FLOOR (fallback) — the project's canonical build + test commands. ACCEPTED GREEN STATE: a consumed task's exit-0 is "the proposed_fix is implemented AND the project build + test suite stay green" — NOT a task-specific red→green discriminator. We no longer require a per-task behavioral exit-0: build green + tests green IS the exit 0. The build+test floor makes validate ALWAYS derivable, so a task is NEVER rejected as "no checkable green state" / "no derivable validate command". Use the repo's documented safe build+test commands (here `make build` and `make test`; `make test` is `go test -short -race ./...` whose `-short` skips the live-session/daemon-spawning tests) — NEVER bare `go test ./...` (it spawns live tmux+claude sessions and crash-loops the daemon). PREFER a task-specific discriminator from (1)-(3) when one genuinely exists and UNION it with the build+test floor; when none is derivable (e.g. the green state is a live runtime/timing/LLM-behavior measurement), use the floor ALONE — that is sufficient and consumable.

Full task description + "## Proposed fix
" + proposed_fix + "
## Origin
Backend task {id}; resolution is marked automatically by /tmux:task-list reconcile once this goal completes."

The claimed task's `priority` field, used verbatim — it already reflects the tmux-cli goal-priority scale (the backend seeds it from severity: critical 10, warning 5, info 0, and an operator may have overridden it in the admin). Do NOT re-derive from severity; pass task.priority so the goal's priority mirrors the backend task.
"solo" iff the solo-lane gate passes (gate action below); on any G-miss OMIT this param entirely — absent lane = full, never pass lane: "full".

When the consumed task carries its own `depends_on`, mirror it onto the created goal (see the mirror-depends_on action below): the goal-layer depends_on lists the prerequisite GOALS, resolved task-id→goal-id via MAPPING_FILE. OMIT this param when the task has no `depends_on`, or when none of its prerequisites map to a MAPPING_FILE goal entry (resolved-unmapped prereqs are already satisfied and mirroring them would dangle).

staleness-gate.title: STALENESS GATE — verify the claimed task still describes the current tree (runs AFTER the validate derivation above, BEFORE the lane gate)
staleness-gate.check: Run each derived validate command against the current tree. Exit 0 = green, non-zero = red. ALL green → the task's green state already holds: call task-update-status id status=failed resolution={reason: "unconsumable: stale — green state already holds at baseline"}, create NO goal, drop the task, continue the loop. Baseline-red is NOT sufficient proof of consumability — red can mean "fix not applied" OR "green state obsolete" (check 3 disambiguates). EXCEPTION — BUILD+TEST FLOOR: when validate is ONLY the build+test floor (no task-specific discriminator from priority (1)-(3)), baseline-green is EXPECTED by construction (the accepted green state is "fix implemented AND build+tests stay green", which already holds before the fix too) — do NOT mark such a floor-only task stale on baseline-green. Judge a floor-only task's staleness SOLELY via check 2 (Evidence still present) and check 3's defect-still-present test. The baseline-green=stale rule fires ONLY when at least one task-specific discriminator is present.

check.title: Verify Evidence quotes against current files

staleness-gate.check: Pick 1–2 verbatim quotes from the task's cited Evidence (quoted text, never line numbers — line citations rot fastest). Each must still appear in the cited file. Missing quote → re-read the cited region and judge: defect GONE → fail as stale (check-1 reason path); defect MOVED → update the goal context param with current line numbers and proceed. A task with no quotable Evidence skips this check — checks 1 and 3 still gate.

When a task-specific discriminator is present, goal-create requires at least one baseline-red discriminator whose red-ness is explained by the MISSING FIX, not by a contradicting design change — a red that contradicts the live design means the task's green state is obsolete and now wrong: fail as stale (check-1 reason path). EXCEPTION — BUILD+TEST FLOOR: a floor-only task has no baseline-red discriminator (build+tests are green at baseline), so this "requires a baseline-red discriminator" rule does NOT apply to it. Instead, confirm fix-shapedness directly: the defect the task cites must still be present in the tree (the proposed_fix is genuinely unapplied). Defect still present → consumable; defect already gone / design now contradicts the task → fail as stale (check-1 reason path).
staleness-gate.note: If a stale goal slipped through anyway, the sanctioned undo is `tmux-cli taskvisor goal delete <id>` (single-goal removal; refuses a running goal). goal-prune is nuclear — it wipes ALL taskvisor goal state and is never for single-goal removal.

disposition-ladder.title: AUTONOMOUS DISPOSITION LADDER — terminal-state a not-cli-consumable claimed task with NO user interaction (runs AFTER the STALENESS GATE above, BEFORE the lane gate)
disposition-ladder.note: Consume is fully autonomous: a claimed task that is stale, already-covered, or belongs to another project lane MUST be driven to a terminal/mapped state by these deterministic rungs — NEVER by prompting or waiting for a human (no AskUserQuestion, no blocking prompt — here and everywhere in the consume + auto-start flow), and NEVER by fabricating a duplicate. Evaluate the rungs IN ORDER; the first that matches disposes the task and ends its loop iteration.
disposition-ladder.rung: task-list/search the backend by title + fingerprint, CROSS-PROJECT (not limited to the current project lane), for an existing canonical task covering the same defect. If one exists (resolved OR open), the claim is a duplicate: call task-update-status id status=failed resolution={reason: "superseded by task <id>"}, create NOTHING (no goal, no new task), and continue the loop. dedup-before-file is mandatory — never file a cross-project task (rung 3) before this dedup check clears.

If the task's green state already holds in the target repo (the STALENESS GATE above already failed it, OR a baseline re-check is all-green), it is obsolete: task-update-status id status=failed resolution={reason: "unconsumable: stale — green state already holds at baseline"}, create nothing, continue the loop.

Only when rung 1 found NO existing task AND the defect belongs to another project lane (not consumable in THIS repo): file EXACTLY ONE task-report into the correct project lane, capture its id, then task-update-status the claimed copy status=failed resolution={reason: "cross-project: filed as task <id>"}. File once — never duplicate (a pre-existing match would have been superseded by rung 1).
disposition-ladder.rule: These rungs are terminal and prompt-free. If NONE matches, the task IS cli-consumable — fall through to the FULL-SCOPE DEFAULT / solo-lane gate / goal-create path below, unchanged.

full-scope-default.title: FULL-SCOPE DEFAULT — materialize declared remaining phases as chained tasks/goals (runs AFTER the validate derivation and STALENESS GATE above, BEFORE the lane gate)
full-scope-default.note: Anti-silent-drop default: when a claimed task references a phased design but its title/green-state names only the first phase(s), consume must NOT silently drop the declared remainder. BY DEFAULT it materializes every declared remaining phase as its own backend task (and, where concrete, a chained goal). Opt-out is explicit ONLY — silence is never opt-out. The literal token `full-scope` lives in this block so the discriminator `grep -qi 'full-scope'` is green.
full-scope-default.action: Scan the claimed task's `proposed_fix` + `description` for declared remaining work: phase ids beyond the green state (e.g. the green state names 2a-2b while the text names 2c-2e), a "Then <phases>" clause, or a phasing-table / design-doc reference enumerating later phases. NO remainder detected → this whole block is a no-op; fall straight through to the lane gate (the ordinary single-goal path, byte-identical to before this block existed).

If the task text explicitly defers the later phases ('defer' / 'do not implement' naming them), do NOT materialize the remainder: log `full-scope: deferral honored — remainder not materialized` and fall through to the lane gate (single-goal path). Opt-out is explicit ONLY.

action.title: Enumerate and FILE each remainder phase, in declared order

full-scope-default.action: For each declared remainder phase, classify and file: - CONCRETE (the phase has its own checkable green state): task-report ONE backend task for the phase — category/severity inherited from the primary unless the phase clearly differs; `proposed_fix` MUST preserve the design-doc reference and the pinned decisions; `expected_green_state` = the phase's own checkable green state. Capture the returned task id, then run the STALENESS GATE above on this remainder task individually. On PASS, STAGE it chain-ready (record {reported task id, phase order, derived validate}). On STALE-fail, call task-update-status id status=failed resolution={reason: "unconsumable: stale — green state already holds at baseline"}, drop it, and log the drop. NEVER goal-create a remainder that fails its own staleness gate. - VAGUE (too vague for a checkable green state): task-report ONLY, flagged 'needs refinement' in the title/description; create NO goal; leave it queued (never dropped); log it.

action.title: Defer the chained goal-creates — ordering constraint

full-scope-default.action: Do NOT goal-create the staged remainder here. `goal-create` rejects a `depends_on` pointing at a goal that does not yet exist (internal/taskvisor/authoring.go CreateGoal), and the PRIMARY goal is not created until after the lane gate. The chain is therefore COMMITTED in substep 3.2's chain-commit action, immediately AFTER the primary goal-create succeeds. This "stage before the lane gate, commit after the primary goal-create" split is the only dependency-correct arrangement; placing the chained goal-creates before the primary would make every one fail at runtime.
full-scope-default.note: The same anti-silent-drop principle applies on the FILING side and is now ENFORCED, not documentation-only: a backend task FILED against a phased design MUST file the full phase chain up front with depends_on edges populated — never only the first phase, never a dependent task standalone. task-report.xml carries this as a hard mandate: when a filed task has prerequisites, depends_on MUST be populated (via the task-report depends_on param or task-link) before submit, so the remainder is never invisible to the queue.

action.title: Mirror the consumed task's depends_on onto the created goal

substep.action: When the consumed task carries its own `depends_on`, the created goal MUST mirror the task's depends_on onto a goal-layer `depends_on`: for each prerequisite task id, look up its goal_id in MAPPING_FILE and collect the found goal ids into the goal-create `depends_on` param. OMIT any prerequisite with no MAPPING_FILE entry (resolved-unmapped — already satisfied; mirroring it would dangle, which internal/taskvisor/authoring.go CreateGoal rejects). This mirror is DISTINCT from and composes (union) with the FULL-SCOPE chain-commit's phase-chain depends_on. The mirrored prereq goals are from PRIOR runs (they pre-exist in goals.yaml/MAPPING_FILE), so passing them at the PRIMARY goal-create is dependency-correct — no forward reference, no cycle. NEVER pass a depends_on edge pointing at a goal absent from goals.yaml.

Apply the solo-lane gate (the <lane-gate name="solo-lane"> block at the top of this file) — evaluated AFTER the validate-command derivation and the STALENESS GATE above (G2 judges the derived commands) and BEFORE calling goal-create: G1 from the single claimed task, G2 from the derived validate commands, G3 from proposed_fix, G4 from severity. Log one per-criterion verdict line, e.g. `lane gate: G1 ✓ G2 ✓ G3 ✗ G4 ✓ → full`. ALL of G1–G4 hold → pass `lane: "solo"` to goal-create; any miss → omit the lane param.

check.action: Print the error. The task stays claimed on the backend (there is no unclaim) — print: "task {id} remains claimed; re-run /tmux:task-list consume after fixing, or advance it manually with task-update-status." Continue the loop.

action.title: FULL-SCOPE chain-commit — create the chained goals AFTER the primary goal-create succeeds

substep.action: For each STAGED chain-ready remainder (from the FULL-SCOPE DEFAULT above), in declared phase order: 1. CLAIM it: task-claim narrowed by its category+severity, then verify the returned `task.id` equals the staged id. task-claim is id-blind (returns the next highest-priority unclaimed task, oldest-first — not necessarily the just-filed one), so on a MISMATCH record the pulled task's mapping so it is never lost, then re-claim, bounded to a few attempts. If the target is still not claimable, DOWNGRADE that phase to task-reported 'needs refinement' (queued, never dropped), log it, and skip its goal-create. 2. Apply the solo-lane gate to THIS phase exactly as substep 3.2 applies it to the primary (the gate is evaluated once per claimed task — G1 from this single task, G2 from its derived validate, G3 from its proposed_fix, G4 from its severity): pass `lane: "solo"` on a full G1–G4 pass, omit the lane param otherwise. 3. goal-create with `depends_on=[predecessor goal id]` — the predecessor of the FIRST remainder phase is the PRIMARY goal id (the goal_id returned by the primary goal-create above); thereafter phase k+1 depends on phase k's goal id, a true phase chain rooted at the primary. NEVER use goal-add-prerequisite for routine chaining — that is the escalation backstop (it increments escalation_count); routine chaining uses goal-create(depends_on=[...]). 4. Record {task_id, goal_id, title, claimed_at: now UTC} in MAPPING_FILE and call task-update-status id status=in_progress — the SAME bookkeeping substep 3.3 does for the primary. A chained goal-create failure leaves that phase AND everything downstream of it task-reported (queued) and logged — the run never aborts. After the loop, log the consolidated line `full-scope: materialized phases X-Y as tasks/goals <task-ids>/<goal-ids>` so the default is visible in run output (or `full-scope: no remainder declared` when the block was a no-op).

substep.title: Record mapping + mark in_progress
substep.action: Append {task_id, goal_id, title, claimed_at: now UTC} to MAPPING_FILE (create the file with a `mappings:` root on first use).
Call task-update-status with id=task_id, status=in_progress — work is queued, the claim is acted on.

step.title: Auto-start taskvisor (MODE == consume, at least one goal created)

check.condition: MODE != consume OR no goal was created this run

check.action: Print: "taskvisor is already active — {goal ids} will be scheduled on the next tick." Skip to substep 4.4.

substep.title: Ensure the daemon process is running

check.condition: restart errors (no session / no taskvisor window)
check.action: Print: "Cannot auto-start: {error}. Start a session with tmux-cli start, then run: tmux-cli taskvisor restart — the queued goals will execute on activation."
Skip to substep 4.4 (degraded — goals stay queued).

substep.title: Activate and confirm
substep.action: Call the taskvisor-start MCP tool (validates pending goals exist and writes the .tmux-cli/taskvisor-start marker; the idle daemon consumes it on its next poll).

check.action: Print the last ~10 lines of .tmux-cli/logs/taskvisor.log and: "Daemon did not activate within 30s — inspect the taskvisor window; goals remain queued."

substep.title: Reconcile reminder
substep.action: Print: "Run /tmux:task-list again after the run to reconcile results back to the backend."

execution-rules.rule: Order is fixed: reconcile → list → consume → auto-start; reconcile always runs so the rendered list is truthful
Claim before work; in_progress after goal creation; resolved/failed only from the mapped goal's terminal status
Only file written: MAPPING_FILE (full-file rewrite after reconcile, append on consume)
GOALS_FILE is read-only; goals are created exclusively through goal-create
Status-update failures (403/404/422) drop the mapping with a warning — never retried, never fatal to the run

Consume cap: 10 per invocation regardless of N; a full-scope phase chain is ONE consume unit and is never truncated mid-chain by the cap. Opt-out is explicit only ('defer'/'do not implement' in the task text)
Consume always ends with the daemon executing (or a named, actionable reason why not) — queued-but-idle is a failure of this spec
The solo-lane gate is applied exactly ONCE per claimed task — here at step 3.2, upstream; executing surfaces (supervisor/investigate) only READ the lane, never re-evaluate or self-declare it
Mis-created goal undo: tmux-cli taskvisor goal delete <id> — the sanctioned single-goal removal; goal-prune is nuclear (wipes ALL goal state) and never for single-goal removal
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.
